seoTitle: Cancel a Payment in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: How to void an incorrect customer payment or cancel a scheduled vendor bill payment in QuickBooks Online without breaking your ledger.
intro: Whether you accidentally cleared the wrong invoice or need to halt a scheduled vendor payment before it processes, QuickBooks Online handles the two situations differently. This guide covers voiding a recorded customer payment to preserve your audit trail, and separately intercepting a scheduled ACH bill payment before it funds.

steps.heading: Find the customer payment
steps.body: Go to Sales, then Customers, and open the profile for the client whose payment you need to cancel.

steps.heading: Open the specific payment
steps.body: In the customer's activity list, click the payment entry you intend to reverse.

steps.heading: Void the transaction
steps.body: Click More at the bottom of the transaction screen, select Void, and confirm the pop-up to zero out the record while keeping the audit trail.

steps.heading: Cancel a scheduled bill payment
steps.body: Go to Expenses, then Bill Payments, find the scheduled ACH row before the funding cutoff, and click View Details.

steps.heading: Halt the scheduled payment
steps.body: Click Cancel This Payment to stop the processing run, then run a Customer Balance Detail report to confirm the invoice returns to Open status.

faq.question: Should I void or delete a payment?
faq.answer: Voiding is preferred since it zeroes out the amount while keeping a visible record for your audit trail, rather than removing it entirely.

faq.question: Can I cancel an ACH payment after it's already processed?
faq.answer: No, you generally need to catch it before the scheduled funding cutoff — once processed, it can't be canceled through this workflow.

faq.question: Will voiding a payment reopen the original invoice?
faq.answer: Yes, voiding the payment updates the customer's accounts receivable balance so the invoice shows as open again.

faq.question: How can I verify a cancellation went through correctly?
faq.answer: Use the Audit Log report to confirm the change, and run a Customer Balance Detail report to check the invoice status.
